preamble: Diphthongization of [o] to [oi] typically occurs through the addition of a high front off-glide, often motivated by contact with palatal consonants or as a spontaneous change.

examples.note: The development of 'oi' in French involved several stages, starting with a diphthongization of [o] or [e] in certain contexts.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
